mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above previous month by > 0.2 percentage points
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal loses predictive power during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through earnings revisions.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns when RSAFS and HOUST diverge, or quarterly forward returns are negative in > 60% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month when the economy operates below full capacity.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth yields negative or flat 20-day forward returns during capacity-constrained periods, or the signal IC drops below 0.01 when capacity utilization exceeds 80%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth momentum persists into short-term equity pricing as higher aggregate demand supports corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show zero correlation with GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth rates, or the signal reverses direction during stagflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation above the 3.5% threshold forces monetary tightening expectations and erodes real corporate margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 65% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5%, or the inflation threshold fails to predict returns when supply shocks dominate.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative returns when wage growth outpaces productivity, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals manufacturing contraction and supply chain bottlenecks, which suppresses short-term equity valuations over the next week during periods of high inventory drawdowns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive despite falling INDPRO, or the mechanism fails to predict returns when inventory-to-sales ratios are below historical averages.
